title: elaeoselinum asclepium subsp. millefolium
intro: Elaeoselinum asclepium subsp. millefolium, also known as Yarrow, is a perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family. It possesses medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent, making it effective in treating wounds, reducing fever, and relieving digestive issues. The health benefits of Elaeoselinum asclepium subsp. millefolium include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and alleviate menstrual cramps, among other benefits. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties, and it can also be applied topically as a salve or poultice to accelerate healing and reduce inflammation.
